summary: Add WordPress REST API publishing integration to SessionForge. Users connect their WordPress site in workspace settings using an application password, then publish blog posts directly from the content editor.

files_to_create.description: Add wordpressConnections pgTable and wordpressPublishedUrl + wordpressPostId columns to posts table
AES-256-CBC encryption/decryption for application passwords using BETTER_AUTH_SECRET
WordPressClient class with testConnection, getCategories, getTags, createPost methods
GET/POST/DELETE route for WordPress connection management
POST route to test WordPress connection and return site info + taxonomy lists
POST route to publish a SessionForge post to WordPress and store the returned URL
WordPress connection settings page with connect/test/disconnect UI
Modal for configuring WordPress publish options (categories, tags, excerpt, status)
Add postId/workspace props and 'Publish to WordPress' menu item
Pass postId/workspace to ExportDropdown and show wordpressPublishedUrl link
Add WordPress nav item — check if this file exists first, may need to create it

key_patterns.auth_check: const session = await auth.api.getSession({ headers: await headers() }); if (!session) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
key_patterns.workspace_ownership: const workspace = await db.select({ id: workspaces.id }).from(workspaces).where(and(eq(workspaces.ownerId, session.user.id), eq(workspaces.slug, slug))).limit(1); if (!workspace.length) return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Workspace not found' }, { status: 404 });
key_patterns.db_import: import { db } from '@/lib/db'; import { posts, workspaces, wordpressConnections } from '@sessionforge/db';
key_patterns.client_component: 'use client'; import { useParams } from 'next/navigation'; import { useQuery, useMutation, useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query';
key_patterns.tailwind_tokens: Use sf-bg-primary, sf-bg-secondary, sf-bg-tertiary, sf-text-primary, sf-text-secondary, sf-border, sf-border-strong, sf-border-focus, sf-accent, sf-accent-dim, rounded-sf, rounded-sf-lg for all UI
key_patterns.api_route_exports: Always export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'; at top of API route files
key_patterns.async_params: { params }: { params: Promise<{ slug: string }> } — always await params before destructuring in Next.js 15
key_patterns.markdown_to_html: import { markdownToHtml } from '@/lib/export'; — already exists, converts markdown string to HTML

existing_dependencies.note: All dependencies below are already in apps/dashboard/package.json — no new npm packages needed
available: lucide-react — Globe, X, Check, ChevronDown icons etc.

wordpress_api_notes.auth: Basic auth: base64(username + ':' + appPassword) — set Authorization header

endpoints.me: GET /wp-json/wp/v2/users/me — test connection, returns { name, url, description }
endpoints.posts: POST /wp-json/wp/v2/posts — body: { title, content (HTML), excerpt, categories (int[]), tags (int[]), featured_media (int), status ('draft'|'publish') }

wordpress_api_notes.wordpress_com_difference: For wordpress.com sites, the API base is https://public-api.wordpress.com/wp/v2/sites/{site-domain}

settings_nav_note: Check if apps/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/[workspace]/settings/layout.tsx exists. If it does, add Globe + WordPress nav link there. If not, add link on the settings/page.tsx directly. The existing settings pages (style, api-keys) don't seem to use a shared layout — check by looking at the pattern.
encryption_key_note: Use process.env.BETTER_AUTH_SECRET as the master key for encrypting app passwords. Hash it with crypto.createHash('sha256') to get a reliable 32-byte key for AES-256-CBC. Store as 'iv_hex:encrypted_hex' string.
